event: maintained on oral pancreatic enzyme therapy for exocrine pancreatic insufficiency
presented to a medical center in India with pain
pain (steady midepigastric, 6 hours duration, dull in nature, radiating through his back, exacerbated by food intake)
hospitalized for pain control with narcotics
returned complaining of continuing epigastric pain
CT scan performed in the emergency department
fatty atrophy of both the pancreatic head and uncinate process
possible obstruction at the level of the neck
marked upstream ductal dilatation of 1.5 cm
again admitted for management of pain secondary to chronic pancreatitis
transferred to the medical intensive care unit
major papilla of Vater spontaneously expelling frank pus
evacuation of more than 5 ml of yellow pus
approximately 20 ml of pus was evacuated
guide wire introduced into the pancreatic duct
assumed acute suppuration of the pancreatic duct (ASPD) secondary to intraductal calculus
contrast enhanced CT scan the following day
inflammatory changes within the fat surrounding the body and tail of the pancreas
dilatation of the pancreatic duct had diminished
blood cultures grew Klebsiella ornithinolytica sensitive to the antibiotics given
transferred from the intensive care unit
follow-up examination at 1 month after discharge: no further complications
follow-up examination at 3 months after discharge: no further complications
